rationale: No qualitative change for Poland this cycle — Iran deal remains unconfirmed, Beirut strikes continue Middle East volatility affecting energy prices indirectly, but no new direct impact on Polish daily life.

top_events.title: Trump says US-Iran deal to be signed Sunday; Tehran casts doubt
top_events.summary: An unconfirmed Iran nuclear deal could ease global energy prices, indirectly benefiting Polish households still sensitive to gas and oil costs. Uncertainty keeps the situation unresolved.

top_events.title: Israel attacks Beirut as Iran deal talks continue
top_events.summary: Israeli strikes on Beirut add volatility to Middle East stability and energy markets, with indirect effects on European energy prices relevant to Polish consumers.

top_events.title: EU set to hold membership talks with Ukraine
top_events.summary: EU formally advancing Ukraine accession talks is a continuation of a known process — no qualitative leap for Poland, though long-term regional stability implications remain.
